title: catatonit -P did not exit once run rootless podman on redhat9
body:
### Issue Description

"catatonit -P" did not exit even my podman process is done 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install rootless podman on redhat9
2. just run podman images
3. ps aux | grep catatonit 
3876835  0.0  0.0   1092     0 ?        S    07:53   0:00 catatonit -P

why this catatonit -P is always there, even  podman no process in running status , how to avoid this ?


### Describe the results you received

catatonit -P
 should be disappeared after my podman job finished 

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

 podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.2.2
API Version:  5.2.2
Go Version:   go1.23.6 (Red Hat 1.23.6-2.el9_5)
Built:        Mon Mar 17 14:03:54 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
we use that process to keep the podman user+mount namespace alive.

We do not currently destroy it when all containers are terminated as it would be more difficult to deal with race conditions when multiple containers are started together, although it should be possible to do it (with some extra cost for the cleanup process as it needs to check if there are other containers running, and `podman unshare` sessions active, and these last ones are not currently tracked anywhere).

If you are sure there are no other containers running though, you can terminate it manually, either by killing it or running `podman system migrate`

Hi, @giuseppe  thanks for the updates, may i know from which version this "catatonit -P" added, since i found on my another ubuntu machine with rootless podman version 3.4.4, i did not seem "catatonit -P" lanuched 

we found this "catatonit -P" during we run podman with IBM LSF scheduler system, LSF job will not exit since it found  "catatonit -P" still there, so we are trying to find if there is any method or any workround we can avoid this ?

also seems this "catatonit -P" will be launched even i just run "podman images " command , this is also expected ?  i thought "catatonit -P"  process will disappeared once podman job finished

The user namespace is the first thing created so yes it is expected that pretty much every podman command creates it.
As @giuseppe mentioned it cannot be killed by podman due to serious race conditions around another podman process starting in parallel. If you know you do not have any other podman process running you can kill it yourself.

older podman versions use podman itself as pause process. The process is called podman pause there I believe.

> although it should be possible to do it (with some extra cost for the cleanup process as it needs to check if there are other containers running, and podman unshare sessions active, and these last ones are not currently tracked anywhere).

I am not sure if this is even doable, you would have to track all podman processes not just containers. podman run might just be started and not yet have created anything but is still part of the current namespace so a cleanup could not kill the process as otherwise contianers will end up in different user namespaces which would be pretty bad.
AFAICS the only way to have some race free is to always hold a global lock (then check and stop if needed) which sounds expensive and would introduce a major bottleneck in my eyes.

> I am not sure if this is even doable, you would have to track all podman processes not just containers. podman run might just be started and not yet have created anything but is still part of the current namespace so a cleanup could not kill the process as otherwise contianers will end up in different user namespaces which would be pretty bad.
> AFAICS the only way to have some race free is to always hold a global lock (then check and stop if needed) which sounds expensive and would introduce a major bottleneck in my eyes.

yes exactly, that is why we don't do it now.  We would need some locking mechanism (it could even be a RW lock, so each user grabs a shared reference and if you want to remove it then you grab the exclusive lock), technically it is possible, but as you said it has a cost.

I'd have solved it in systemd, but my proposal was rejected, and the offered workaround doesn't really work for us: https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/16318

so that means currently, we may has no method to avoid this "catatonit -P" ?  :(
since under same user , he will run a lot of different podman jobs , we can not simply kill "catatonit -P" ,since all podman job share one  "catatonit -P" process

If you are actively using podman why would you need to kill it? This is a very small process that should consume very little memory and basically no cpu. In fact if you are actively using podman this process is good to have because we can quickly rejoin the existing userns which is faster than having to reexec podman each time in order to create the userns.

because we have a cluster ,which has more than 100 machines, we need use scheduler system(IBM LSF) to run job on this cluster, if that "catatonit -P"  is there , then scheduler system think the job is still in running status , will not finish

How does the scheduler even know about this process? The process gets forked in the background, closes the std streams and we move it to another systemd cgroup so it should generally not be observable as interactive process.

Or you can just run any podman command on startup before you schedule jobs to start the pause process outside the scheduler.

In any case I don't think we change the podman design so IMO we should just close this issue.
